title: Defining Emergence
concept: An ability is emergent if it is absent in smaller models (performance at random chance) but suddenly appears once the model size scales past a threshold.

exercises.prompt: Welcome! Today we are learning about 'Emergence' in AI. What do you think this concept is about?

options.text: How scaling up model size can suddenly unlock new skills.
How to make an AI model smaller and faster.
How to program an AI using hard-coded rules.

exercises.explain: Emergence is all about how scale (adding more parameters or data to make a model larger) unlocks sudden, unexpected new behaviors.

cards.title: More is Different
cards.body: Have you ever noticed how a tiny group of ants can't do much, but a giant colony can build complex underground cities? In AI, size changes everything. When AI models get big enough, they suddenly gain mind-blowing new skills out of nowhere!

cards.title: What is Emergence?
cards.body: Emergent abilities are skills that do not exist at all in smaller AI models (they perform no better than random guessing), but suddenly appear and work well once the model grows past a certain size threshold.

cards.title: The Wetness of Water
cards.body: Think of water molecules. One single molecule isn't wet, and it cannot flow like a river. But when you bundle billions of them together, wetness and flowing suddenly 'emerge'. Similarly, a small AI model can't solve riddles at all, but when it grows big enough, the ability to solve riddles suddenly appears!

cards.title: The Math Breakthrough
cards.body: A small language model with 1 billion parameters gets 0% on a complex math word puzzle exam (the same score as random guessing). But when we test a 100-billion-parameter version of the exact same model, it suddenly scores 85%! The math ability emerged.

cards.title: Myth: Growth is Always Gradual
cards.body: Many people think that AI models get steadily better at everything as they grow. But that's a myth! For emergent abilities, smaller models are completely useless (performing at random chance) until they suddenly hit the magic size threshold.

cards.title: Gradual vs. Emergent Growth
cards.body: Let's compare how normal abilities and emergent abilities grow as an AI model gets bigger. Normal abilities improve step-by-step, while emergent abilities show up all at once.

right.text: Flat at zero or random guessing, then suddenly shoots up to high accuracy at a specific size threshold.

left.text: Gradual improvement. As the model grows, performance gets slightly better at each step.

exercises.prompt: Before an AI model reaches the size threshold for an emergent ability, how does it perform on that task?

options.text: It is already highly accurate and gets even better.
It is completely absent and performs at random chance.
It is slightly better than human experts.
It works perfectly but runs very slowly.

exercises.explain: In small AI models, emergent skills are completely absent, meaning the model's performance is no better than random guessing (random chance).

exercises.prompt: True or False: We can easily predict when an emergent ability will appear just by looking at how well a very small model performs.
exercises.explain: Emergent abilities cannot be easily predicted by looking at small models because the performance stays flat at random chance until the threshold is crossed.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how emergent abilities appear in larger AI models.
exercises.sentence: An ability is emergent if it suddenly appears only after the model size scales past a certain ______.
exercises.explain: Emergent abilities do not show up gradually; they appear suddenly only after the model's size passes a specific scale threshold.

exercises.prompt: Which of these scenarios describes an emergent ability?

options.text: A model's translation score goes from 10% to 12% to 15% as it grows.
A model scores 10% (random guess) at all small sizes, then suddenly jumps to 80% at 100B parameters.
A model scores 10% at all sizes and never gets any better.

exercises.explain: Option B represents emergence because the ability is absent (random guess rate of 10%) at small sizes, but suddenly jumps to 80% once the model gets large. Option A is gradual growth, and Option C is just bad performance.

exercises.prompt: You are analyzing a chart of a model's performance. The line stays flat at 0% accuracy for a long time, and then suddenly shoots up to 90% when the model size reaches 100 billion parameters. What is this phenomenon called?

options.text: The model's accuracy gets slowly and steadily better with every single new parameter added.
The model suddenly gains a brand new ability only after crossing a specific size threshold.
The model gets worse at simple tasks as it grows larger.
The model is born with all its abilities from the very beginning, even at tiny sizes.

exercises.explain: Emergence is characterized by a sudden jump from random chance performance to high accuracy once a specific model size (threshold) is reached. It cannot be predicted just by looking at small models.
